title: 'Will not leave Mamata in difficult time': Shatrughan Sinha picks Team Didi amid TMC turmoil
content: Amidst a party exodus, TMC MP Shatrughan Sinha pledged loyalty to Mamata Banerjee, stating he wouldn't abandon her during challenging times, recalling her support for him.  This declaration follows reports of his alleged involvement in forming a separate faction.  Earlier, Sinha also congratulated Prime Minister Narendra Modi on his 12 years in office, calling him a "friend and guide."

title: Meenakshi Natarajan row ends in BJP’s 3-0 Rajya Sabha sweep in MP amid Congress protest
content: The BJP secured three Rajya Sabha seats in Madhya Pradesh unopposed after the Congress nominee's nomination was rejected. Despite legal challenges and protests by the Congress, the Election Commission and Supreme Court did not intervene in time, leading to the BJP's victory. This outcome concluded a politically charged battle marked by allegations of horse-trading and constitutional conspiracy.

title: Anvay Dravid, son of legendary Rahul Dravid, named in India U-19 squad
content: Anvay Dravid, son of former India captain Rahul Dravid, has been selected for the India Under-19 squad touring Sri Lanka. The BCCI announced the squads on Thursday, with Anvay named as one of the wicketkeepers for the one-day matches. This selection follows his impressive performance in the Vinoo Mankad Trophy.

title: SpaceX IPO tomorrow: Could Elon Musk become world's first trillionaire? Decoding the math
content: Elon Musk may soon become the world's first trillionaire. This is due to the upcoming initial public offering of his company SpaceX. The IPO is expected to be the largest in history. If valuations hold true, Musk's personal fortune could surge past the trillion-dollar mark. This event could reshape global wealth rankings and create new millionaires.

title: Google 'refuses' funding from Trump administration for Quantum Computing; here's why
content: Google declined a $2 billion US government quantum computing grant, citing restrictive conditions that would hinder its rapid progress.  Despite this, the tech giant advocates for increased government investment in foundational research and global talent acquisition to maintain US competitiveness against China in this crucial field. Other companies, like PsiQuantum, have embraced such funding.

title: ‘Human shield’ on India border: Bangladesh opposition to launch protest over alleged 'push-ins'
content: Bangladesh's Jamaat-e-Islami and opposition parties are protesting alleged Indian "push-ins" and border killings. They claim thousands were pushed back and 19 Bangladeshis killed by Indian forces. Opposition leaders demand stronger border security and criticize India's stance, emphasizing the impact on Bangladeshi lives and sovereignty.

title: Buyer claims Rs 1.65 crore Defender failed its speed claim; court orders full refund
content: A luxury car buyer has won a major consumer dispute against Jaguar Land Rover India. The company was ordered to refund ₹1.65 crore with interest for a defective Defender SUV. The vehicle failed to meet advertised performance, had missing features, and underwent unauthorized chassis modifications, leading to the commission's strong ruling.

title: Microsoft warns staff: Don't touch Claude Fable 5, lawyers are still reading fine print
content: Microsoft has blocked its own employees from using Anthropic's newly launched Claude Fable 5 inside GitHub Copilot, citing unresolved concerns over a 30-day data retention policy tied to the Mythos-class model. The Verge reports Microsoft's legal team is still reviewing the changes, even as paying Copilot and Foundry customers already have access. Other Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention rules. Anthropic has not offered a workaround.

title: 'Choose Abhishek or me': Kalyan Banerjee's blunt message to Mamata amid turmoil in TMC
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee issued an ultimatum to Mamata Banerjee, demanding she choose between her nephew Abhishek Banerjee and senior party workers. Banerjee also announced he would no longer represent Abhishek in legal matters, citing disrespect and arrogance from the latter. This comes amid internal party tensions following electoral setbacks.

title: 'Rs 370 biryani' remark: NCW summons comedian Pranit More, web developer Himanshu Jangra
content: The National Commission for Women is investigating a stand-up comedy show in Gurugram. A viral video showed remarks allegedly glorifying sexual coercion. The NCW has asked the Haryana Police for an action report within seven days. Comedians Pranit More and Himanshu Jangra have been summoned. The NCW stressed that consent is non-negotiable.

title: Palantir CEO’s message to tech CEO: Don’t be surprised if your employees turn against you
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp cautions tech leaders against publicizing AI-driven job cuts, warning it could provoke backlash from employees, voters, and policymakers. He suggests such pronouncements risk fueling opposition and strengthening calls for tech regulation. Karp himself acknowledges Palantir uses AI for efficiency but plans to boost revenue without significant workforce expansion.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity benchmarks closed lower on Thursday, impacted by global weakness, rising crude oil, and Middle East tensions. IT stocks saw significant selling, while banking and pharma offered some support. Markets experienced volatility throughout the session, reflecting investor caution amid geopolitical concerns and FII outflows.

title: 'Inappropriate to abandon a ship in troubled waters': Babul Supriyo on leaving TMC
content: TMC leaders Babul Supriyo and Shatrughan Sinha have reaffirmed their loyalty to Mamata Banerjee amidst a party exodus. Both stated they would not abandon the party during its troubled times, emphasizing Banerjee's past support for them. This comes as the TMC faces a significant crisis following electoral defeat and a rebellion weakening its legislative strength.

title: 'All soldiers of Congress': Pilot plays down Gehlot's remarks, says he has always shown affection
content: Sachin Pilot downplayed Ashok Gehlot's recent criticism, emphasizing party unity and affection. Pilot highlighted national challenges like exam paper leaks and rising prices, vowing to strengthen the INDIA bloc against the BJP. He also criticized the Rajasthan government and alleged institutional pressure on the Election Commission.

title: ‘Pushpa of Falta’, TMC leader Jahangir Khan paraded in shorts after arrest
content: Trinamool Congress leader Jahangir Khan, also known as ‘Pushpa’, was paraded through Falta by police following his arrest near the India-Nepal border. Khan, who unsuccessfully contested the Falta assembly seat, was remanded to five days police custody in connection with seven extortion and other charges.

title: 'By next World Cup, India must have a backup': Raina on Pandya injuries
content: Suresh Raina has urged India to find a dependable backup for all-rounder Hardik Pandya, citing his persistent fitness issues. Pandya's recent injury, ruling him out of the Afghanistan series, underscores the team's need for depth in pace-bowling all-rounders ahead of the next ODI World Cup. Identifying and nurturing an alternative is crucial to avoid vulnerability.

title: Precision and dominance: Navy unveils crest of Project 17A frigate Dunagiri
content: India's INS Dunagiri, a new stealth frigate, boasts advanced multi-domain combat capabilities. Named after a Himalayan peak, it features BrahMos and Barak-8 missiles for formidable offensive and defensive power. This advanced warship, built by GRSE, signifies a major leap in indigenous naval technology and is set for commissioning soon.

title: Wipro Rs 15,000 crore buyback opens - what it means for retail investors
content: Wipro's Rs 15,000 crore share buyback is now open, offering shareholders Rs 250 per share, a significant premium over the current market price. The offer, valid until June 17, presents a potential arbitrage opportunity for retail investors. However, analysts suggest limited gains, with acceptance ratios expected around 20%, and caution about risks associated with unaccepted shares.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' OTT release: When and where to watch Akshay Kumar-starrer horror comedy
content: Priyadarshan's horror-comedy, Bhooth Bangla, starring Akshay Kumar, is set for a Netflix premiere on June 12 after a theatrical run. The film, which earned over ₹181 crore net in India, follows siblings inheriting a haunted palace for a destination wedding. Fans are excited for the digital release, with discussions also touching upon its VFX and the director-actor duo's previous hits.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi told to stay away from professional managers
content: Former India pacer S Sreesanth has lauded teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who recently became the youngest player selected for an Indian men's team. Sreesanth emphasized Suryavanshi's commitment and perseverance, urging the 15-year-old to focus solely on cricket and avoid distractions from professional managers, trusting his family to handle off-field matters.

title: Haryana guest teacher suspended days after joining Cockroach Janta Party protest
content: A guest teacher in Haryana's Rohtak district has been suspended after participating in a protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. Sulekha Dalal's suspension, effective June 8, followed a viral video of her addressing the Cockroach Janta Party rally. Dalal stated her participation was due to concerns over recruitment exam irregularities affecting her son.

title: MT Jalveer comes under attack off Oman coast; over 20 sailors on board
content: India is closely monitoring a maritime incident near Oman's Shinas port, following recent attacks on commercial vessels. This comes after three Indian sailors died in a US-led strike on another ship. The Indian Embassy in Muscat is coordinating with local authorities for details, while New Delhi has lodged a strong protest with the US over the earlier incident.

title: Dubai-based Indian banker conquers Mount Everest after life-changing accident
content: At 40, Shradha Gupta wasn't a mountaineer but a banking executive. After a life-altering accident, she stopped waiting for the 'right time' and began climbing, reaching Everest just three years later. Her journey proves ambition isn't finite and extraordinary achievements start with a single step.

title: Punjab doctor 'who helped fund' husband's hospital found dead
content: A doctor's husband, an eye surgeon, faces charges of abetting suicide after his wife was found dead. Her parents allege extramarital affairs, violence, and a massive Rs 2.5 crore loan taken in her name without consent. The couple had been living separately following alleged assault attempts and threats.

title: Boost for litchi exports! Assam’s GI-tagged Tezpur litchi reaches Dubai, Singapore
content: Assam's prized Tezpur litchi has made its international debut with the first export consignment reaching Dubai. This milestone, facilitated by APEDA, highlights efforts to boost Northeast agricultural exports. The GI-tagged fruit, celebrated for its exceptional taste and aroma, fetched farmers higher prices, promising enhanced livelihoods and opening new global markets for this century-old cultivation legacy.

title: 3 Indian sailors killed after US strike on oil tanker near Strait of Hormuz
content: Two Indian sailors have died and a chief engineer is missing after a commercial vessel, MT Settebello, was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz. While 21 of the 24 Indian crew members were rescued, the incident raises serious concerns for seafarers in the volatile Gulf region. India has formally protested the attack, emphasizing the need to protect maritime workers.

title: Water cannons, fires: Belfast protest against immigrants turns violent - watch
content: Violent clashes erupted in Belfast for a second consecutive night as masked rioters attacked police with projectiles, prompting authorities to deploy additional officers and water cannon.

title: How the 1994 FIFA World Cup changed soccer in America and why 2026 could be bigger
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is set to kick off today. The former United States soccer star Landon Donovan believes the tournament could leave a lasting impact on the sport in the country. As the United States hosts matches alongside Canada and Mexico, Donovan expects millions of young fans to be inspired by the event. He feels the excitement generated by the competition could encourage a new generation of children to take up soccer and dream of playing at the highest level.

title: Woman claims skin worsened after Rs 3.5 lakh treatment, court dismisses plea
content: A Bengaluru consumer commission dismissed a woman's complaint against SkinLab, stating negligence in cosmetic treatment requires expert medical evidence. The complainant alleged worsening acne and hair growth after Glutathione IV therapy and LHR. The clinic countered that she signed consent forms and underwent concurrent treatments, which could cause adverse reactions. The commission found no proof of negligence without expert opinion.

title: 'Homemaker builds nation': SC values domestic care loss at Rs 30,000 every month
content: The Supreme Court has recognized homemakers as 'nation builders' and ruled that the loss of their domestic care services should be a separate head of compensation in motor accident claims. The court fixed a notional monthly income of Rs 30,000 for assessing this loss, emphasizing the substantial economic and social value of their contributions to families and society.

title: Day after Kuki hostages’ release, 6 abducted Nagas found dead in Manipur
content: Six Naga villagers, abducted four weeks ago by Kuki groups, were found dead in Kangpokpi district, Manipur. This discovery has reignited ethnic tensions, prompting a 24-hour shutdown call by the United Naga Council. The UNC demands justice, including abrogating pacts with Kuki militants and declaring KNF-P a terrorist outfit.

title: Delhi weather: 41°C on paper, but it feels like 48°C
content: Delhi sweltered under a scorching 48.2 degrees Celsius 'feels-like' temperature on Wednesday. While an orange alert for rain and thunderstorms has been issued for Thursday, relief might be temporary as humidity persists.  Temperatures are expected to drop significantly by Friday, offering a much-needed respite from the intense heat.

title: 10 bullets in 5 seconds: CCTV captures chilling murder outside Haryana gym
content: Fitness trainer Kapil Redhu was shot dead outside his gym in Haryana's Hansi by two assailants on a motorcycle. The brazen daylight execution, captured on CCTV, saw the attacker fire nine bullets at close range. The Lawrence Bishnoi gang later claimed responsibility for the murder, alleging Redhu had ignored prior warnings.

title: Isha Rikhi confirms marriage with Badshah, shares photo: 'My husband owes an explanation'
content: Punjabi actress Isha Rikhi has confirmed her marriage to rapper-singer Badshah during a social media Q&A. Speculation intensified after wedding ceremony photos surfaced earlier this year. Rikhi playfully addressed fan questions about their social media following and shared a picture with Badshah, ending months of rumors and delighting fans with the first official confirmation.

title: Watch - Blasts rock Robat Karim near Tehran as US targets alleged IRGC command centre: Report
content: The US conducted fresh strikes targeting Iran's IRGC command-and-control infrastructure, escalating tensions. These "self-defense strikes" hit military surveillance, communication, and air defense sites across Iran, including near Tehran and in Hormozgan province. The attacks follow previous US strikes after Iran allegedly downed a US helicopter.

title: How the Indian Navy's new GPS jammers could fool enemy missiles, drones and navigation systems
content: India's Ministry of Defence has procured 20 advanced GNSS jammers, featuring 75% indigenous content, for Rs 449 crore. This strategic acquisition bolsters maritime security and electronic warfare, enabling the Indian Navy to counter threats by disrupting adversary satellite navigation systems like GPS. The move addresses rising interference incidents and enhances operational resilience across all domains.

title: How the C-295 transport aircraft will add value to IAF
content: India's aerospace sector achieved a significant milestone with the first flight of the India-assembled C-295 aircraft. This $3.5 billion program with Airbus will see 40 aircraft assembled locally, boosting domestic manufacturing and replacing aging An-32s. Additional orders for the Navy and Coast Guard highlight the C-295's versatility and India's growing defense production capabilities.

title: Monsoon 2026 tracker LIVE: India expected to receive below-average rain over next two weeks, report says
content: The conditions are favourable for advancement of southwest monsoon into some parts of Jharkhand within the next two to three days, the IMD said in a bulletin.

title: Monsoon triggers surge in fever, infectious diseases across Kerala’s central Travancore region
content: Health authorities warn of a growing threat from shigella, H1N1, dengue, leptospirosis and other waterborne illnesses

title: Bharathiraja passes away LIVE | Actor's funeral held with State honours
content: A towering personality in the film industry, Bharathiraja was fondly known as Iyakkunar Imayam (Pinnacle among Directors)

title: Congress nominee Praveen Chakravarty declared elected unopposed to Rajya Sabha
content: Barring Mr. Chakravarty’s nomination, the nominations of the remaining 12 independent candidates were rejected during scrutiny on June 9

title: Kerala on Nipah alert after Kozhikode man’s preliminary test turns positive
content: Lab results of his body fluid samples sent to National Institute of Virology in Pune for Nipah infection are awaited

title: Two shot dead, houses burnt by armed assailants in Manipur's Kamjong
content: Security forces have reached the remote village, but the identity of the perpetrators is yet to be known

title: T.N. government to move most of the property registration process online within six months: Minister
content: Minister for Commercial Taxes and Registration D. Logesh Tamilselvan told The Hindu that once the system is implemented, people will have to visit the Sub-Registrar Office only for the final authentication process

title: Health dept. tightens surveillance following confirmation of two Shigella cases in Kollam
content: Both the infected children, aged 6 and 8, are currently stable and receiving treatment, prompting officials to emphasise strict hygiene practices and the consumption of safe drinking water to prevent further spread of the infection.

title: Young woman’s suspicious death in Thoothukudi turns out to be murder; brother, mother arrested
content: The 18-year-old was initially said to have died by suicide; probe revealed she was murdered by her family for being in a relationship with a man from Salem; police rule out caste killing

title: Choose between Abhishek and me: Kalyan Banerjee issues ultimatum to Mamata
content: Amid Trinamool Congress imbroglio, the senior party leader accuses the party national general secretary of ‘arrogance and fluctuating mindset’; the senior advocate declared that he would remove himself from all legal matters and court petitions concerning Abhishek Banerjee

title: Kejriwal questions PM Modi's message to Trump, says India must safeguard national interest
content: ‘PM Modi is saying ‘thank you’ to Trump. Thank you for exactly what? For attacking ships with Indians on board and killing Indian nationals?’ AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal posted on X

title: ‘No ID, no entry’ rule raises questions by pub and bar owners in Karnataka about implementation, fake IDs
content: Several industry stakeholders told The Hindu that while the rule itself is not new, there is little clarity on the operational aspects of enforcement

title: Despite monsoon setting in, heatwave conditions forecast to persist in Telangana till weekend
content: The highest temperature on Wednesday (June 10, 2026) was 43°C and 18 districts recorded maximum temperature exceeding 42°C. Thunderstorms are predicted in 18 districts for Friday.

title: West Bengal CID searches residence of Mamata Banerjee, Trinamool office
content: Search by the State agency comes in connection with alleged forgery of signatures of party MLAs; Trinamool leaders slam action as ‘political vendetta’, Bengal Minister says no one is above the law

title: Ram Mandir donation row: Mahant seeks impartial probe; Piyush Goyal says Akhilesh's claims lack credibility
content: Amid the ongoing row over alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple, Mahant Das said that if any wrongdoing had taken place, it must be probed

title: Sukhendu Sekhar Ray distances himself from TMC rebel group
content: While endorsing the revolt as ‘the right thing to do’, veteran leader says he is yet to decide whether to retire after a 59-year-long political career, or join another party

title: ‘Not an era of war’: In Bulgaria, Jaishankar calls for dialogue and diplomacy to resolve conflicts
content: EAM Jaishankar is on a two-nation tour of Bulgaria and Finland aimed at advancing India's engagement with European partners and strengthening bilateral cooperation across sectors

title: Shatrughan Sinha vouches support for Mamata Banerjee amid allegations of joining rebel group of TMC
content: ‘My principle is very clear: when Mamata ji stood by me in my difficult times, it is now my duty to stand by her in this moment as well,’ says Lok Sabha MP Shatrughan Sinha

title: Kerala rains: Orange alert for two districts on June 12
content: Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Thrissur, Kannur and Kasaragod districts have been put on yellow alert for Friday

title: CPI not part of DMK alliance in Tamil Nadu, says Veerapandian
content: “The current situation is not conducive for an alliance. In the coming days, when there are local body and other elections, the CPI will decide its stand. The CPI takes its own political decisions,” he said

title: India continues to move forward on its growth trajectory amid global uncertainty: PM Modi at NITI Aayog meeting
content: The PM, chairing the 11th meeting of the Governing Council of NITI Aayog, said India has signed free trade agreements with many countries to create new opportunities for growth and exports

title: Signature forgery case: Calcutta High Court directs Abhishek Banerjee to appear before CID
content: The HC also granted an interim protection to Abhishek Banerjee, directing that the CID cannot take any coercive action as long as he cooperates with the investigation

title: Cockroach Janta Party to agitate in Pune, release manifesto recommending educational reforms
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke and its supporters plan to travel across the country, intending to build credibility by addressing one issue at a time

title: CBI coordinates return of two fugitives from Georgia and Thailand
content: In the first case, accused Vainket Garg was wanted by the Haryana Police in multiple cases of murder, attempted murder, extortion, organised crime and possession of legal arms and he was brought from Georgia to India on Thursday; in the other case, Ganesh Balaso Kale was deported from Thailand as he was wanted in connection with a cybercrime fraud

title: Committed to strengthening Kerala’s tourism sector, says Tourism Minister
content: P.C. Vishnunadh emphasises the importance of private investment and announced plans for comprehensive development initiatives and marketing strategies.

title: Firmly opposed to attacks on merchant shipping: India tells UNSC amid ongoing Iran conflict
content: Parvathaneni said India is firmly opposed to attacks on merchant shipping, as many of its nationals are prominent in its global workforce

title: India summons U.S. Deputy Chief of Mission after second ship attack in three days
content: “We condemn the attack on the commercial vessel Settebello off the coast of Oman,” the Ministry of External Affairs said in a statement.

title: Nipah virus: Here’s what you need to know
content: Nipah virus has been reported multiple times in Kerala in the past. Strictly following the guidelines issued by health authorities is a must to curb the spread of misinformation

title: Home Minister reviews policing, traffic, cybercrime and narcotics control measures
content: Priyank Kharge said the Home Department is contemplating the launch of a Bengaluru Urban Mobility Plan involving stakeholders such as the GBA, BMRCL, Transport Department, Traffic Police and other civic agencies to formulate coordinated strategies for decongesting the city’s traffic.

title: EAC-PM recommends targeted splitting of seats for delimitation, shows model allowing 50% rise for all large States
content: The advisory council says it assembled a dataset on elections to LS seats from 2009 to 2024 to estimate a ‘statistical relationship’ between voter turnout, constituency size, and five compositional features of the constituency; the model results in increasing the size of the Lok Sabha to 824 seats and it is broadly in line with what the Centre had suggested in April, when it brought the Delimitation-related Bills but failed to pass them in Parliament

title: Odisha govt. alleges two inquiry reports went missing from CMO during Naveen Patnaik tenure
content: Home Department seeks FIR over disappearance of reports on Swami Laxmanananda Saraswati killing and SUM Hospital fire incident from the Chief Minister’s Office

title: Kerala HC grants State two weeks to respond to charges against Waqf Board in PIL by BJP’s Shone George
content: Shone George alleged that Waqf Board was functioning in violation of mandates under United Waqf Management, Empowerment, Efficiency and Development Act

title: Supreme Court to hear Meenakshi Natarajan’s plea against rejection of RS candidature on June 12
content: Supreme Court declined to grant any interim relief preventing the declaration of the election result, observing that courts ordinarily do not interfere once the election process is underway

title: Nipah outbreak | Connection between environment and epidemic in focus
content: The three localities from where the Nipah virus originated in Kozhikode district over the past five years have similar geograpahy and flora

title: Digital arrest fraud: Bengaluru homemaker duped of ₹98.4 lakh during two-month ordeal
content: The fraudsters allegedly told the woman that her identity documents had been misused in a money laundering case, and that multiple criminal cases had been registered against her

title: Mettur Dam not to be opened for delta irrigation on June 12
content: The government will decide on the release of water for irrigation if the region receives heavy rainfall in the coming days

title: Watch: Trinamool Congress crumbles | Implications for West Bengal and national politics
content: Ever since the Trinamool Congress lost power in the Assembly elections after being in power for three terms, the party seems to be imploding. The rebellion has spread within the legislature party as well as the parliamentary grouping as well. To analyse this crisis, we are joined by Sankhadip Das, proprietor editor of TheWall.in.

title: Three missing Indian seafarers onboard MT Settebello confirmed dead: Sonowal
content: Union Shipping Minister Sarbananda Sonowal said he has directed officials to ensure immediate repatriation of the rescued crew members and swift return of the mortal remains of the deceased for their final rites

title: 3 injured in two suspected elephant attacks in Kerala’s Palakkad
content: The incidents occur a day after a 65-year-old man was killed in a wild elephant attack in Wayanad district

title: Watch: Modi becomes India’s longest serving elected PM, NDA celebrates
content: The Union Cabinet congratulated Prime Minister Narendra Modi for becoming India’s longest-serving elected Prime Minister after completing 4,399 consecutive days in office. Leaders including President Droupadi Murmu, Home Minister Amit Shah and Defence Minister Rajnath Singh also welcomed the milestone.

title: Kerala Civil Supplies dept faces over ₹3,000 crore in debt, may need long-term revival: Minister
content: Anoop Jacob attributes mounting liability to previous LDF government’s decision not to revise price of 13 subsidised items during its 10-year tenure since 2016

title: Arguing before a trial court is like playing a Test match, while appeals are like IPL matches: Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan dissent with a view taken by Justice Vikram Nath of Supreme Court during his stint as a judge of Allahabad High Court

title: How do you intend to appoint Director of Prosecution? Madras High Court asks T.N. Govt
content: The petitioners had sought a direction to the the State government to frame statutory rules governing the appointments in the directorate of prosecution and not to make any appointment to the top posts in the interregnum

title: SC uses special powers to set aside POCSO conviction to let couple live ‘peacefully’ as spouses
content: Victim married the convict when she became an adult and wished to continue the relationship; top court cautions that relief was granted due to the peculiar facts of this case and will not operate as a precedent

title: Women's Body Summons Comedian, Man Behind 'Rs 370 Ki Biryani' Row
content: The NCW said it would continue to monitor the matter closely and expected prompt and appropriate action from the concerned authorities.

title: "War Crime": Father Of Indian Killed In US Attack On Ship Off Oman Coast
content: Rajesh Sharma, Aditya's father, who last spoke to his son on Sunday, urged the government to take a strong stand against the US.

title: All 4 BJP Candidates Elected Unopposed To Rajya Sabha From Gujarat
content: The winning candidates are Rajubhai Shukla, Mansinh Parmar, Mukeshbhai Rathwa and Jitendra Kanjariya.

title: M Kharge, 3 Others Elected 'Unanimously' To Rajya Sabha from Karnataka
content: Members of the Karnataka Legislative Assembly were scheduled to vote in this election at Vidhana Soudha.

title: UK Defence Minister Resigns, Criticises Starmer's Plans To Keep Nation Safe
content: British defence minister John Healey quit on Thursday in a dispute over military spending, accusing Prime Minister Keir Starmer of failing to commit the government resources that are needed to defend the country at a time of heightened threat.

title: Brother, Mother Kill Woman Over Her Relationship In Tamil Nadu, Arrested
content: Political parties have described the incident as a possible honour killing and urged the government to enact a special law to prevent such crimes.

title: More Trouble For Abhishek Banerjee, Tripura Court Summons Him In 2021 Case
content: Abhishek Banerjee allegedly assaulted policemen after the West Bengal assembly election and amid the COVID-19 pandemic

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Markets Close On Muted Note As Oil Rises After Fresh US Strikes On Iran
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent futures rose to $94.56 a barrel, while US WTI crude rallied to $91.73 a barrel.

title: Biggest Investing Mistakes In 2026: Why Investors Keep Falling For Bad Stocks
content: According to experts, even experienced market participants are falling into traps despite spending years analysing markets.

title: Indians In UK Living In 'Fear' After 'Racist' Arson Attacks In Belfast
content: Videos on social media showed a crowd of protesters hurling objects toward police vehicles, and officers responding by firing water cannons at them.

title: 'Trinamool Exodus Reflects Public Dissatisfaction': Minister Jabs Mamata Banerjee
content: CR Patil alleged that leaders who once projected Mamata Banerjee as politically invincible are now abandoning the party because they fear its declining public support and uncertain future.

title: Third Ship With  Indian Sailors On Fire Near Oman
content: The incident was confirmed by the Indian Embassy in Oman, which said it was closely monitoring the situation and coordinating with the local authorities for further details.

title: "I Worry Something Will Go Wrong": Anthropic CEO On AI
content: Anthropic co-founder and CEO Dario Amodei is very vocal about his fears that AI could get out of hand.

title: How Indians Are Training AI Robots To Take On Household Jobs In The Future
content: Artificial intelligence chatbots and image generators crunch reams of digital data, but building systems to navigate real-life environments is more challenging.

title: "Stay Safe": UK Cops Warn Of Unrest In Belfast Over "Hit List" Of Migrants
content: Police warned that those creating or sharing the list could be committing a criminal offence and deployed 200 additional officers to help prevent further violence.

title: Maharashtra woman ploughs field in bullock’s place; govt steps in with a bull
content: videograb shows Hausabai Gaikwad stepping into the harness, taking place of the bull killed by lightning on June 5 . landless farmer Kashinath stepped into a dust-choked field in Bombali, about 63km from Latur . his wife houseabai replaced the animal the family could no longer afford to lose .

title: Alive by chance, shattered by fate: AI crash sole survivor battles scars
content: sole survivor Viswashkumar is still struggling with physical pain, bereavement, financial difficulties and psychological problems . he survived the crash to be with his son, Divang, now five, who was starting school for the first time and also to get medical treatment . his younger brother, Ajay (35), was sat on the plane with him and did not get out alive .
